Elementi.Opis: Articulare is the point of intersection the dorsal contours of the processus articularis mandibulare and os tempoarle.
A crossection of a line tangent to the posterior border of the rami and a line tangent to the inferior border of the mandible.
Lower most point of the contour of the chin.
Sella is the midpoint of sella turcica or hypophyseal fossa or pituitary fossa.
Nasion is the most anterior point of the frontonasal suture in the middle.
Sella-Nasion line (SN, Nasion-Sella line, NSL) A frequently used cephalometric reference line representing the anterior cranial base. A line joining points S and Na.
A is the deepest point on the curved bony outline between the anterior nasal spine and prosthion.
The deepest midline point on the mandible between infradentale and pogonion.

Basion is the median point of the anterior margin of the foramen magnum can be located by following the images of the slope the inferior border of the basilar part of the occipital bone to its posteri

The incisal edge of the maxillary central incisor.
Incision superius incisalis Isi UII

The root apex of the most anterior maxillary central incisor.
Incision superius apicalis Isa UIA
A line on the cephalometric radiograph representing an imaginary plane at the level of the occlusion. There are various different definitions

Incision inferius incisalis is the incisal edge of the most prominent mandibular central incisor.
Incision inferius incisalis Iii LII

The root apex of the most anterior mandibular central incisor; if this point is needed only for defining the long axis of the tooth.
Incision inferius apicalis Iia LIA
The most anterior inferior point on the bony chin in the midsagittal plane. (midsagittal)

Y-axis (Growth axis)
A line connecting points sella and gnathion.
Porion is the most superior point of the external auditory meatus.
The deepest point on the infraorbital margin.
An anthropological horizontal plane described on dry skulls as passing through the lowest point in the floor of the left orbit and the highest point on the margins of the external auditory meati.
Most anterior point of mandibular symphysis.

The most posterior inferior point on the outline of the angle of the mandible. It may be determined by inspection or it can be constructed by bisecting the angle formed by the intersection of the mand

Mandibular plane (MP, Mandibular line, ML)
A line representing the plane passing through the mandibular borders (bilaterally). It can be drawn in two different ways.
PNS is the intersection of a continuation of the anterior wall of the pterygopalatine fossa and the floor of the nose.
Anterior nasal spine is the tip of bony anterior nasal spine in the midline or median plane.

Palatal plane (ANS-PNS, PP, Nasal line, Nasal floor, Spinal plane)
A line joining PNS and ANS.

Mandibular plane (MP, Mandibular line, ML)
A line representing the plane passing through the mandibular borders (bilaterally). It can be drawn in two different ways.
Mandibulary occlusal plane. A line joining the cusp tips of the mandibular first permanent molars to the incisal edge of the mandibular central incisors.
The concave or retruded point in the tissue overlying the area of the frontonasal suture.
The most prominent or anterior point on the soft tissue chin in the midsagittal plane.
The pterygoid point is the lower lip of the foramen rotundum. Most posterior point on the outline of the pterygopalatine fossa.

Xi Point (Xi) approximate for Inferior alveolar foramen (Ricketts)
A constructed point located at the geographic center of the ramus.

Protruberance Menti (PM) or Supra pogonion (Ricketts)
A point where the curvature of the anterior border of the symphysis changes from concave to convex.
The point at which the nasal septum between the nostrils merges with the upper cutaneous tip in the midsagittal plane.
The most anterior point on the margin of the upper membranous lip.

DC Point (Ricketts)
Center of the neck of the condyle on the Basion Nasion line.
A line tangent to the soft tissue chin and the upper lip, introduced by R. A. Holdaway for assessment of the soft tissue profile.
The most prominent or anterior point of the nose.

The point of greatest concavity in the midline of the upper lip between subnasale and labrale superius.
Soft A Ss-Soft

Maxillary central incisor is the most labial point on the crown of the maxillary central incisor.
+1 +1 face
The point of greatest concavity in the midline of the lip between labrale inferius and soft tissue pogonion.
The tangent point to each medial condylar border of a line drawn parallel to each mandibular body line.

Elementi.Naziv: Distal side of upper first molar

Elementi.Opis:
CC Point Ricketts
(Cranial Center) Crossing of the facial axis with the BaN plane
Mandibular central incisor is the most labial point on the crown of the mandibular central incisor. -1L facet

Elementi.Naziv: Distal side of lower first molar

Elementi.Opis:
Stomion superius
The lowest midline point of the upper lip. (midsagittal) if lips are apart.
Sts
The most prominent or anterior point in the midsagittal plane of the forehead at the level of the superior orbital ridges.
Maxillary oclussal plane. A line joining the cusp tips of the maxillary first permanent molars to the incisal edge of the maxillary central incisors.
Alar crease junction is the most posterior point of the curved line formed by the alar crease.

Stomion inferius
The highest midline point of the lower lip. (midsagittal) if lips are apart.
Sti
Prosthion is the lowermost anterior point of alveolar process of pre-maxilla in the midline between two maxillary central incisors.
The infradentale is the point of transition from the crown of the most prominent mandibular medial incisor to the alveolar projection.
Ratio of distances of mandinular incisor edge to NB plane and pogonion to NB plane
'238. A-NP II cHP' - '239. Ls'' strain II cHP'
Ratio of Mand. and cranial base length
Id -infradentale Pg - Pogonion constructed tangent mandibular line
maxillary occlusal plane Mx Nasal line ANS PNS
mandibular occlusal plane ML Me-tGo tangent
Tangent gonion constructed according to ABO
Point for tracing plane tangent to inferior border of mandible according to ABO
Plane tangent to the inferior border of the body of the mandible - according to ABO.
Point for tracing tangent to ramus according to ABO
Line tangent to the postrior border of ramus ramal according to ABO
Gonion point constructed according to ABO
Mandibular plane constructed according to ABO
Absolute distance upper incisal edge to ANS.
place point AppGo ppproximately to gonion position in order to align Md. silhouette to the mandible
Point defining Occlusal plane between incisors

instead of using the occlusal plane for the perpendicular projections of A and B, we use a line traced between the middle point of -1/+1 and the intersection point of the mandibular plane (Downs) with
Intersection of the superior border of the greater wing of the sphenoid bone and the lateral orbital margin
Intersection of the superior border of the greater wing of the sphenoid bone and the lateral orbital margin
Most lateral aspect of the zygomatic arch
the highest point on the superior aspect of the nasal septum
Most lateral aspect of the zygomatic arch
Most lateral point on the nasal cavity
Most lateral point on the nasal cavity
center of the intersection of the nasal septum and the palate
Most inferior point on the mastoid process
Most inferior point on the mastoid process
midpoint on the curvature at the gonial angle
The highest point in the antegonia/notch
midpoint of the inferior border of the mental protuberance
The highest point in the antegonia/notch
midpoint on the curvature at the gonial angle
intersection of the lateral contour of the maxillary alveoler process and the lower contour of the mxillozygomatic process of maxilla
intersection of the lateral contour of the maxillary alveoler process and the lower contour of the mxillozygomatic process of maxilla
Midpoint on the incisal edges of the maxillary incisors
Crest of the alveolus between the maxillary central incisors
Midpoint on the incisal edges of the mandibular incisors
Crest of the alveolus between the mandibular central incisors
Maxillary first molar Midpoint on the buccal surface of the maxillary first molar
Mandibular molar Midpoint on the buccal surface of the mandibular first molar
Maxillary first molar Midpoint on the buccal surface of the maxillary first molar
Mandibular molar Midpoint on the buccal surface of the mandibular first molar
